description_long: This is a circular stone temple with a colonnade of tall Corinthian columns supporting a wide entablature and a shallow, red-tiled dome roof. The structure sits atop a raised stone platform, accessed by a broad staircase leading to the main entrance. The exterior walls are clad in large, light-colored stone blocks, and the columns feature ornate capitals with acanthus leaf motifs. The roof is constructed from reddish terracotta tiles, giving a distinct contrast to the pale stone below. The temple's open colonnade allows visibility into the interior, which appears spacious and likely open to the air. Decorative elements are restrained, focusing on classical column capitals and clean architectural lines.
description: A circular stone temple with Corinthian columns, a red-tiled dome roof, and a raised platform accessed by a wide staircase. The structure features pale stone walls and ornate capitals, with a classical, open colonnade design.
description_view_0: Shows the circular colonnade and red-tiled dome roof, with the stone platform visible but the entrance and stairs not in view.
description_view_1: Reveals the side of the temple, highlighting the colonnade and the start of the wide staircase and entrance platform.
description_view_2: Direct frontal view of the temple, showcasing the main staircase, entrance, and symmetrical arrangement of columns.
description_view_3: Displays the opposite side of the temple, with the staircase and entrance platform extending outward and the colonnade wrapping around.
attribute_ornamentation: moderate density, Corinthian capitals with acanthus leaf motifs, smooth stone surfaces, minimal additional decoration
attribute_entrance_design: Wide, straight staircase leading to a columned portico with no visible doors, framed by Corinthian columns and a simple entablature
